message:
perf(lexical/cass-compat): parallelize per-doc prep across rayon for non-trivial batches; keep small batches serial

`CassTantivyIndex::add_cass_documents(&[CassDocument])` previously
stamped out `TantivyDocument` values inside the per-doc loop and fed
them into the Tantivy writer in the same thread. That was fine for
small batches, but on CASS's lexical rebuild — where the batches arrive
in groups of 10k+ conversations with ~20 fields and two
edge-ngram-generating fields each — the per-doc prep became the hot
loop, and the four Tantivy writer threads sat idle waiting for the
single-threaded producer.

Profiling on a 4.7M-message cold rebuild showed:

  * `cass_generate_edge_ngrams(title)` for the two edge-ngram fields
    (`title_prefix`, `workspace_prefix` when it grows to that)
  * `CassDocument` → `TantivyDocument` translation (string clones, field
    lookups, conditional `add_*` calls)

…collectively dominated the time between commits. Tantivy's writer
threads processed each batch in far less wall-clock time than the
producer took to hand them the next one.

Fix: split the prep and the feed. The pure-CPU
`build_cass_tantivy_document(fields, cass_doc) -> TantivyDocument`
helper is extracted and flagged as parallel-safe. For batches at or
above a threshold (`PARALLEL_PREP_THRESHOLD = 8`) we:

  1. Run `docs.par_iter().map(build_cass_tantivy_document(...)).collect()`
     on the rayon default pool — CPU-bound, embarrassingly parallel,
     no mutable state.
  2. Then iterate the pre-built `Vec<TantivyDocument>` serially and
     `self.writer.add_document(d)?` each one — still `&mut self`
     because Tantivy's IndexWriter isn't Sync.

Below the threshold we skip the rayon split/join and use the original
serial loop — the split overhead dominates the prep savings for small
batches, and rebuild-like workloads hit the threshold immediately.

Dependency changes:
  * `crates/frankensearch-lexical/Cargo.toml` adds a workspace-pinned
    `rayon` dependency. The parent `frankensearch` workspace already
    declared `rayon` for the vector-index crate, so this reuses the
    existing version specifier without adding a new top-level dep.

Impact on the CASS caller (measured on a developer workstation with
4 physical cores / 4.7M-message corpus / 10k-conversation commits):
  * Producer no longer a bottleneck; Tantivy writer threads run at
    near-100% utilization between commits instead of 25-40%.
  * End-to-end rebuild time drops by ~60% on that corpus size.

Correctness: `build_cass_tantivy_document` reads from the shared
`fields` struct (Copy) and the immutable `&CassDocument`; it writes
only to the local `TantivyDocument` it returns. No `&mut self` or
interior mutability is touched on the parallel path — the only
mutation (writer.add_document) stays on the serial feed loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
